{{Chess Opening Theory/Position |Classical variation |eco=[[Chess/ECOA|A01]] |parent=[[../|Nimzowitsch-Larsen attack]] }} == 1...d5 · Classical variation == Black's controls the centre and opens up the queenside bishop. This prevents White from playing e4 or Nc3, as the pawn will be taken or the knight kicked respectively. White can respond with a variety of moves, where most of them lead to the same result, like Bb2 and Nf3. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}
